CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value VARCHAR(100));
UPDATE test_table SET value = 'Updated' WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, ref_id INT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'Reference 1');
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id, description) VALUES (2, 1, 'Reference 2');
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id, description) SELECT id, id, 'Nested ' || value FROM test_table;
UPDATE another_table SET description = 'Updated Reference' WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value LIKE 'Test%');
CREATE TABLE final_table (final_id INT PRIMARY KEY, final_value VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO final_table (final_id, final_value) VALUES (2, (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = 1));
